error_message: Too many open connections from pool
known_fix:
1. Increase the connection limit in db-config.yaml to 200 and apply: `kubectl patch configmap event-db-config -p '{"data":{"maxPoolSize":"200"}}'`.
2. Terminate idle DB connections: `docker exec event-db psql -c "SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state='idle';"`.
3. Restart the event-service deployment to reinitialize pools: `kubectl rollout restart deployment/event-service`.

error_message: timeout: failed to acquire cluster coordination lock after 20s
known_fix:
1. cordon and drain the affected Kafka node
   kubectl cordon kafka-0 && kubectl drain kafka-0 --ignore-daemonset --timeout=30s
2. Restart the producer service deployment to refresh its connections
   kubectl rollout restart deployment/analytics-producer
3. Apply a temporary lock timeout override via environment variable
   kubectl set env deployment/analytics-producer MAX_LOCK_WAIT=15s --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
4. Verify health by checking consumer lag metrics
   curl -s http://analytics-service/health | grep lag
